data.title: DigitalOcean Holdings Shares Fall on $700M Public Offering
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Shares of DigitalOcean Holdings fell after the company said it would raise $700 million through a public offering of shares.

Shares fell 9% to $77.31 in after-hours trading Tuesday after closing at $84.92. At close, the stock had more than doubled over the past year.

The cloud-infrastructure company said it will use proceeds from the offering to expand its infrastructure capacity to meet demand, pay down an existing term loan, and for other general corporate purposes.

The underwriters for the offering have a 30-day option to buy an additional $105 million of stock from the company.

JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley, and Bank of America Securities are joint book-running managers.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Devon Energy Extends Credit Facility to 2031 and Removes 10 bps SOFR Spread
data.description:
Devon Energy entered into a First Amendment to its Amended and Restated Credit Agreement to extend the facility’s maturity and lower borrowing costs. The amendment pushes the maturity to March 24, 2031, renews Devon’s option to seek up to three one-year extensions with majority lender consent, and removes the 10 basis point credit spread adjustment to SOFR-based rates. The company expects the changes to enhance liquidity and reduce interest expense.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Amendment to Amended and Restated Credit Agreement (revolving credit facility)
  • Counterparty: Bank of America, as administrative agent, and other lenders
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 24 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Until Mar 24 2031, with up to three one-year extensions subject to consent
  • Reason: Extend maturity and reduce borrowing costs

Original SEC Filing: DEVON ENERGY CORP/DE [ DVN ] - 8-K - Mar. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: CytoDyn Extends $57M Convertible Notes to 2029; 5% Rate, $1M Monthly Stock Payments
data.description:
CytoDyn amended and extended two secured convertible promissory notes totaling $57.0 million, pushing their maturities to April 2029. The company will make $1,000,000 in monthly payments in common stock across both notes, priced at the lower of the prior closing price or the five-day average. The annual interest rate on each note was reduced to 5%, improving the overall cost of capital and liquidity profile. The extensions were agreed with the Noteholders on Mar 24, 2026.

Agreement 1: CytoDyn Extends $28.5M Convertible Note to 2029; Rate Cut to 5%, Stock Payments

  • Agreement type: Amendment and extension of secured convertible promissory note
  • Counterparty: Noteholders
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 24 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: 36 months (to Apr 5 2029)
  • Reason: Extend maturities and reduce interest expense

Agreement 2: CytoDyn Extends Second $28.5M Convertible Note to 2029 With 5% Interest

  • Agreement type: Amendment and extension of secured convertible promissory note
  • Counterparty: Noteholders
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 24 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: 36 months (to Apr 23 2029)
  • Reason: Extend maturities and reduce interest expense

Original SEC Filing: CytoDyn Inc. [ CYDY ] - 8-K - Mar. 24, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Cingulate Launches $100 Million At-The-Market Offering With A.G.P./Alliance Global Partners
data.description:
Cingulate entered into an at-the-market (ATM) Sales Agreement with A.G.P./Alliance Global Partners to sell up to $100 million of its common stock from time to time. Shares may be sold on the Nasdaq Capital Market or through negotiated transactions under an effective shelf registration. A.G.P. will act as sales agent and receive a 3.0% commission on gross proceeds. The program provides Cingulate with flexible access to capital, and may be suspended or terminated at any time.

  • Counterparty: A.G.P./Alliance Global Partners
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 24 2026 / Mar 24 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Raise capital flexibly via at-the-market sales

data.title: Caring Brands Raises $3.6M PIPE, Adds Registration Rights and Retires 6.25M Insider Shares
data.description:
Caring Brands secured $3.6 million through a PIPE financing, issuing Series A Convertible Preferred Stock and 9.47 million five-year warrants at a $0.40 exercise price. The preferred stock converts at $0.40 per share and includes ownership caps and a 19.99% issuance limit without shareholder approval; the investor also has an additional $4.0 million investment right with pricing protections. The company entered a Registration Rights Agreement to register the resale of conversion and warrant shares. Caring Brands will use $3.075 million of proceeds to repurchase 6.25 million insider shares, cutting outstanding shares to approximately 8.51 million, with the balance for general corporate purposes.

Agreement 1: Caring Brands Raises $3.6 Million in PIPE; Issues Series A Preferred and 9.47M Warrants

  • Agreement type: Securities Purchase Agreement for PIPE financing (Series A Preferred and Common Warrants)
  • Counterparty: Accredited Investor Purchaser
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 19 2026 / Mar 19 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Strengthen liquidity and fund share redemption and working capital

Agreement 2: Caring Brands Signs Registration Rights Deal to Register Preferred Conversion and Warrant Shares

  • Agreement type: Registration Rights Agreement for resale registration
  • Counterparty: Accredited Investor Purchaser
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 19 2026 / Mar 19 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Provide resale liquidity to investor

Agreement 3: Caring Brands to Retire 6.25M Shares From Insiders Using $3.075 Million of PIPE Proceeds

  • Agreement type: Share Redemption Agreements to repurchase insider-held common stock
  • Counterparty: Certain Insiders (Dr. Glynn Wilson; Brnan John; NovoDX)
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 19 2026 / Mar 19 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Reduce share count and align insider holdings

Original SEC Filing: Caring Brands, Inc. [ CABR ] - 8-K - Mar. 24, 2026

data.title: BCP Investment Raises $50 Million in 7.50% Notes Due 2029 to Refinance Debt
data.description:
BCP Investment entered into financing agreements supporting a $50 million offering of 7.50% notes due 2029, with proceeds earmarked to repay $40 million of 5.25% notes due 2026 and to pay down other indebtedness. The company signed a Note Purchase Agreement with purchasers on March 20, 2026 and a Sixth Supplemental Indenture with U.S. Bank Trust Company on March 24, 2026 to establish terms and covenants. The notes pay interest semi-annually and feature a make-whole call prior to April 24, 2029 and a par call on or after that date, improving liquidity and extending maturities.

Agreement 1: BCP Investment Raises $50 Million in 7.50% Notes Due 2029 to Refinance Debt

  • Agreement type: Note Purchase Agreement for 7.50% notes due 2029
  • Counterparty: Purchasers named in the Note Purchase Agreement
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 20 2026 / Mar 20 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Through Sep 24 2029
  • Reason: Refinance 2026 notes and reduce other indebtedness

Agreement 2: BCP Investment Executes Sixth Supplemental Indenture for 7.50% Notes Due 2029

  • Agreement type: Sixth Supplemental Indenture governing 7.50% notes due 2029
  • Counterparty: U.S. Bank Trust Company
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 24 2026 / Mar 24 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Through Sep 24 2029
  • Reason: Set terms and covenants for the new notes

Original SEC Filing: BCP Investment Corp [ BCIC ] - 8-K - Mar. 24, 2026

data.title: MPTI: Revenue up 11.2% and backlog up 61.8% in 2025, supporting strong future growth
data.description:
Revenue grew 11.2% year-over-year to $54.4M in 2025, with net income at $8.4M and backlog up 61.8% to $76.4M. Gross margin declined to 44.4% due to tariffs and product mix, but strong cash inflows from warrant exercises support future growth.

data.title: PPL says FERC sets New England transmission ROE at 9.57%, refunds ordered
data.description:
PPL said FERC set a new ROE methodology for New England transmission owners, establishing a base ROE of 9.57% and maximum with incentives of 12.09%, retroactive to Oct. 16, 2014.

Key Highlights:

  • FERC issued Opinion No. 594 setting NETOs' base ROE at 9.57% and max with incentives at 12.09%, retroactive to Oct. 16, 2014.
  • FERC ordered refunds with interest for certain periods affected by the decision, impacting Rhode Island Energy.
  • PPL and Rhode Island Energy are evaluating next steps, including potential coordination with other NETOs and possible appeal.
  • PPL does not expect the Order to have a material impact and reaffirms its 2026 EPS forecast of $1.90–$1.98 and 6%–8% annual EPS growth target.

Original SEC Filing: PPL Corp [ PPL ] - 8-K - Mar. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Janus Henderson Lifts Merger Price to $52 Per Share in Amendment With Jupiter
data.description:
Janus Henderson entered into Amendment No. 1 to its Agreement and Plan of Merger with Jupiter Company and Jupiter Merger Sub, raising the cash consideration to $52.00 per share. The amendment also revises termination-related payments, including a fixed $118.2 million expense reimbursement if the required vote fails, and increases potential termination fees. It permits quarterly dividends up to $1.00 per share for quarters starting on or after July 1, 2026, subject to key approvals, and allows post-vote employee rollover opportunities. The merger would result in Janus Henderson becoming a wholly owned subsidiary of Jupiter Company.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Amendment No. 1 to Agreement and Plan of Merger
  • Counterparty: Jupiter Company and Jupiter Merger Sub
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 24 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Until closing of merger
  • Reason: Enhance deal terms and advance merger closing

Original SEC Filing: JANUS HENDERSON GROUP PLC [ JHG ] - 8-K - Mar. 24, 2026

data.title: CEA Industries says YZi Labs' request to fix record date is deficient, blocks consent solicitation
data.description:
CEA Industries announced its board found YZi Labs’ request to set a record date for a consent solicitation deficient and will not set a record date.

Key Highlights:

  • Board reviewed YZi Labs’ request and determined the Request Letter omitted material disclosures required by the bylaws.
  • Because the request is deficient, the Board has not set a record date and YZi Labs may not proceed with its proposed consent solicitation.
  • Request omitted number of BNC shares held by YZi Labs, related persons and nominees, and financial relationships of at least six nominees.
  • Board says omissions prevent evaluation of potential conflicts and nominee independence; stockholders need take no action now.
  • Company will file a consent revocation statement and related materials with the SEC and will review any corrected request for compliance.

Original SEC Filing: CEA Industries Inc. [ BNC ] - 8-K - Mar. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Wesdome Appoints Tyler Mitchelson as Chief Operating Officer
data.description:
Toronto, Ontario--(Newsfile Corp. - March 24, 2026) - Wesdome Gold Mines Ltd. (OTCQX: WDOFF) ("Wesdome" or the "Company") is pleased to announce the appointment of Tyler Mitchelson as its full-time Chief Operating Officer ("COO"), following his appointment as interim COO as announced on January 20, 2026.

"Following a comprehensive recruitment process, we are delighted to officially welcome Tyler to Wesdome's executive leadership team," said Anthea Bath, President and Chief Executive Officer. "Tyler brings a proven track record in mining operations, combining rigorous operational discipline with a truly collaborative leadership style that will be a great asset to our operations. His relentless focus on driving operational excellence through enhanced systems and processes positions us for sustainable, resilient growth and accelerated success. We're already seeing tangible results during his interim period – a strong indicator of the positive impact Tyler will have on Wesdome."

Mr. Mitchelson previously served as Senior Vice President, Copper Growth at Teck Resources Limited from 2022 to 2025 where he led the development of a world-class portfolio of copper and zinc growth projects. Prior to Teck, Mr. Mitchelson held key roles at several mining companies, including Chief Executive Officer of Metallurgical Coal and Group Head of Integration / Business Planning at Anglo American and President and Chief Executive Officer at Royal Nickel Corporation. He also held senior positions at Vale Inco Limited and Inco Limited. Mr. Mitchelson graduated from the University of Manitoba with a Bachelor of Commerce (Honours) and obtained his Chartered Accountant designation from the Institute of Chartered Accountants of Manitoba.

About Wesdome

Wesdome is a Canadian-focused gold producer with two high-grade underground assets, Eagle River in Northern Ontario and Kiena in Val-d'Or, Québec. The Company's primary goal is to responsibly leverage its operating platform and high-quality brownfield and greenfield exploration pipeline to build a value-driven mid-tier gold producer.

For more information:

  • Raj GillSVP, Corporate Development & Investor RelationsPhone: +1.416.360.3743E-mail: invest@wesdome.com
  • Trish MoranVP, Investor RelationsPhone: +1.416.564.4290E-mail: trish.moran@wesdome.com

data.title: S&P Dow Jones Indices Announces Change to the S&P/TSX Canadian Dividend Aristocrats Index
data.description:
(21:15 GMT) *S&P Dow Jones Indices Announces Change To The S&P/TSX Canadian Dividend Aristocrats Index >SPGI
(21:15 GMT) Press Release: S&P Dow Jones Indices Announces Change to the S&P/TSX Canadian Dividend Aristocrats Index

TORONTO, March 24, 2026 /CNW/ - S&P Dow Jones Indices announces the following index change to the S&P/TSX Canadian Dividend Aristocrats Index as a result of the monthly dividend review. Change will be effective prior to the open of trading on Wednesday, April 1, 2026.

 
S&P/TSX Canadian Dividend Aristocrats Index -- April 
 1, 2026 
                  Symbol         COMPANY 
DELETED           GSY            goeasy Ltd.

For more information about S&P Dow Jones Indices, please visit www.spdji.com

ABOUT S&P DOW JONES INDICES

S&P Dow Jones Indices is the largest global resource for essential index-based concepts, data and research, and home to iconic financial market indicators, such as the S&P 500(R) and the Dow Jones Industrial Average(R) . More assets are invested in products based on our indices than products based on indices from any other provider in the world. Since Charles Dow invented the first index in 1884, S&P DJI has become home to over 1,000,000 indices across the spectrum of asset classes that have helped define the way investors measure and trade the markets.

S&P Dow Jones Indices is a division of S&P Global , which provides essential intelligence for individuals, companies, and governments to make decisions with confidence. For more information, visit www.spdji.com.

SOURCE S&P Dow Jones Indices LLC.

/CONTACT:

Copyright CNW Group 2026

data.title: Playground Ventures Announces Appointment of Director
data.description:
Toronto, Ontario--(Newsfile Corp. - March 24, 2026) - Playground Ventures Inc. (the "Company"), is pleased to announce that Mr. Damien Reynolds has been appointed as a director of the Company, effective immediately.

Mr. Reynolds is a Canadian entrepreneur with multiple decades of experience in the small public capital markets. While his primary focus has been mineral exploration globally, he is also involved in many tech startups and investments.

In addition, the Company would like to announce the resignation of Mr. Harrison Reynolds as a director of the Company. Management and the board of directors would like to thank Mr. Reynolds for his contributions to the Company and wishes him well in his future endeavours.

For further information, please contact:Jon Gill, Chairman and Interim Chief Financial Officer

Tel: 416-361-1913

data.title: Highrock Resources Upsizes Private Placement
data.description:
Toronto, Ontario--(Newsfile Corp. - March 24, 2026) - Highrock Resources Ltd.  ("Highrock" or "the Company") is pleased to announce that, further to its press release of February 24, 2026, it has upsized its non-brokered private placement to up to 2,000,000 units (the "Units") in the capital of the Company at a price of $0.05 per Unit for gross proceeds of up to $100,000 (the "Offering").

Each Unit shall be comprised of one common share in the capital of the Company (each, a "Common Share") and one whole Common Share purchase warrant (each, a "Warrant"). Each Warrant entitles the holder thereof to acquire one Common Share at a price of $0.075 per Common Share until the date that is two (2) years from the date of issuance. Gross proceeds raised from the Offering will be used for working capital and general corporate purposes.

Closing of the Offering is subject to receipt of all necessary corporate and regulatory approvals, including the approval of Canadian Securities Exchange. All securities issued in connection with the Offering will be subject to a hold period of four months plus a day from the date of issuance and the resale rules of applicable securities legislation.

This press release does not constitute an offer to sell or a solicitation of an offer to buy the securities in the United States. The securities have not been and will not be registered under the United States Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the "U.S. Securities Act") or any state securities laws and may not be offered or sold within the United States or to U.S. Persons as defined under applicable United States securities laws unless registered under the U.S. Securities Act and applicable state securities laws or an exemption from such registration is available.

About Highrock Resources Ltd.

Highrock Resources is a Canadian exploration company focused on strategic and precious metals in North America.

For further information, please contact:

Derrick Dao

Chief Executive Officer

+1-437-677-5075

data.title: Cayson Acquisition 10-K: $0 Revenue; Net Income $1.64M (2025), $0.475M (2024)
data.description:
Cayson Acquisition reported no operating revenue for the periods covered in its Form 10-K, reflecting that the company remains a blank‑check vehicle with activities limited to formation, IPO and target search. Net income was $1.64 million for the year ended December 31, 2025 (which includes $2.536 million of interest income from the trust) and $0.475 million for the period May 27, 2024–December 31, 2024 (which includes $0.752 million of interest income from the trust). These results reflect investment income from the trust account rather than operating earnings.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0 — the company has not generated operating revenues to date.
  • Net Income (Year ended Dec 31, 2025): $1.64 million (includes $2.536 million interest from trust).
  • Net Income (May 27, 2024–Dec 31, 2024): $0.475 million (includes $0.752 million interest from trust).
  • Net Income Per Share: Not provided in the source data.

Business Highlights

  • Business model: Cayson Acquisition is a Cayman Islands blank‑check company formed to effect a merger, share exchange, asset acquisition, stock purchase, reorganization or similar business combination, with a focus on opportunities in Asia.
  • Operations: No operating business activity or operating revenues; activities to date have focused on IPO completion, organization, administrative arrangements and target search and due diligence preparations.
  • Capital formation: Completed an IPO and related private placement and established a trust account to hold proceeds that will be used to finance a future business combination.
  • Corporate setup: Put in place a public listing, an administrative support agreement and directors & officers insurance and processes for evaluating potential targets.
  • Outlook & risks: Intends to use trust funds to complete a business combination; the filing notes substantial doubt about the company’s ability to continue as a going concern if a business combination is not completed within the prescribed combination period.

Original SEC Filing: Cayson Acquisition Corp [ CAPN ] - 10-K - Mar. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Fox Tungsten Announces Resignation of Director
data.description:
(TheNewswire)

Vancouver, British Columbia - March 24, 2026 -TheNewswire - Fox Tungsten
Ltd. (“FoxTungsten” or the “Company”)announces that Grant McAdam has resigned fromthe Company’s Board of Directors.  

Investment funds managed by Waratah Capital AdvisorsLtd. (“Waratah”) have indicated an intention to participate in theoffering, as previously disclosed in theCompany’s news release dated March 24, 2026,on a pro rata basis. As a result of Canadian securities lawrequirements which prohibit registered advisers such as Waratah frominvesting in an issuer when one of its investment team members servesas an officer or director of the issuer, Grant McAdam has resignedfrom the board of directors of the Company, effective immediately.However, Waratah has confirmed that it intends to exercise its rightto nominate a replacement to the board of the Company.

The Company would like to thank Mr. McAdam for hiscontributions to the Board and wishes him all the best in his futureendeavours.

On behalf of the Board of Directors,

“Stephen Gray”

President and Chief Executive Officer

FOR FURTHER INFORMATION, PLEASECONTACT:

Stephen Gray

Email: steve.gray@foxtungsten.com

Phone: 416 898-7247

About Fox Tungsten Ltd.

Fox Tungsten is focused on making new discoveries and buildingresources in proximity to infrastructure on the Company’s100-percent-owned portfolio of diversified metals projects in BritishColumbia.

Projects include the high-grade Fox Tungsten deposit, the Silverbossmolybdenum-copper-gold-silver project adjacent to Glencore’s closedBoss Mountain molybdenum mine and the adjacent Hen-Art-DL gold andsilver project.

On November 7, 2024, Fox Tungsten announced the closing of the sale ofthe Highland Valley Copper Project to Metal Energy Corp. (TSX:V MERG)(“Metal Energy”). Fox Tungsten holds  2,347,220 common shares ofMetal Energy.

Fox Tungsten is committed to responsible mineral resource development. The Company’s priority is to build and sustain mutually beneficialrelationships with Indigenous Communities in the territories in whichthe Company explores.

Additional information relating to Fox Tungsten Ltd. may be obtainedor viewed on the SEDAR+ website at www.sedarplus.ca or on the Company’swebsite at www.foxtungsten.com.

data.title: Curis 10-K: $9.44M Revenue, $(0.58) EPS — Net loss improved on one-time gain
data.description:
Curis reported fiscal year revenue of $9.443 million and a net loss per share of $(0.58), with results reflecting a decline in revenue following the sale of Erivedge and an improved net loss driven in part by a $27.189 million gain on release of liability. Operating expenses remained elevated at $42.345 million, while loss from operations narrowed to $(5.713) million versus a much larger loss in the prior year. Management indicates funding remains a primary focus despite completing a January 2026 PIPE to extend operations.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues, net: $9.443 million, a 13% decrease versus the prior year primarily due to the sale of Erivedge.
  • Operating expenses: $42.345 million (includes R&D $28.254 million and G&A $14.046 million).
  • Loss from operations: $(5.713) million, improved compared with $(44.542) million in the prior year.
  • Net loss: $(7.582) million, an 83% improvement year-over-year that includes a $27.189 million gain on release of liability.
  • Net loss per common share (basic and diluted): $(0.58); weighted average shares outstanding: 13.164 million.

Business Highlights

  • Clinical focus: Strategic pivot to emavusertib combination programs, including trials in relapsed/refractory primary central nervous system lymphoma (PCNSL) with ibrutinib and a planned Phase 2 chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL) study with zanubrutinib; dosing for the CLL study is expected in the first half of 2026.
  • Regulatory pathway: CHMP and FDA feedback indicated the single-arm TakeAim study could support conditional marketing authorization/NDA based on objective response rate endpoints, with confirmatory trials required.
  • Revenue and channel shift: The company ceased the Erivedge royalty stream following its sale, reducing recurring revenue as the business refocused on development-stage assets.
  • Operational runway and funding: Cash was insufficient to fund operations beyond 12 months at the filing date; the company completed a PIPE in January 2026 to extend its runway, but securing additional funding remains a key operational priority.

Original SEC Filing: CURIS INC [ CRIS ] - 10-K - Mar. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Cohen & Steers Closed-End Funds Declare Distributions for April, May and June 2026
data.description:
NEW YORK, March 24, 2026 /PRNewswire/ — The Board of Directors of the Cohen & Steers Closed-End Funds announced today the monthly distributions for April, May and June 2026, as summarized in the charts below:

 
                                                                     Monthly 
Ticker   Fund Name                                                    Dividend 
-------  ----------------------------------------------------------  --------- 
FOF      Cohen & Steers Closed-End Opportunity Fund, Inc.            $0.087 
         Cohen & Steers Limited Duration Preferred and Income Fund, 
LDP       Inc.                                                       $0.131 
PSF      Cohen & Steers Select Preferred and Income Fund, Inc.       $0.126 
         Cohen & Steers Tax-Advantaged Preferred Securities and 
PTA       Income Fund                                                $0.134 
RFI      Cohen & Steers Total Return Realty Fund, Inc.               $0.080 
RLTY     Cohen & Steers Real Estate Opportunities and Income Fund    $0.110 
RNP      Cohen & Steers REIT and Preferred and Income Fund, Inc.     $0.136 
RQI      Cohen & Steers Quality Income Realty Fund, Inc.             $0.090 
UTF      Cohen & Steers Infrastructure Fund, Inc.                    $0.165 
-------  ----------------------------------------------------------  ---------

Distributions will be made on the following schedule:

 
Month  Ex-Dividend/    Payable Date 
        Record Date 
-----  --------------  -------------- 
April  April 14, 2026  April 30, 2026 
May    May 12, 2026    May 29, 2026 
June   June 9, 2026    June 30, 2026 
-----  --------------  --------------

Cohen & Steers Infrastructure Fund, Inc. has increased its monthly distribution by $0.010 per share, to $0.165 per share. The Fund's monthly distribution has been adjusted to reflect current market conditions.

Cohen & Steers Tax-Advantaged Preferred Securities and Income Fund, Cohen & Steers Real Estate Opportunities and Income Fund, Cohen & Steers Limited Duration Preferred and Income Fund, Inc., and Cohen & Steers Select Preferred and Income Fund, Inc. (each, a "Fund" and collectively the "Funds") pay regular monthly cash distributions to common shareholders at a level rate that may be adjusted from time to time. Each of these Funds' distributions reflect net investment income and may also include net realized capital gains and/or return of capital. Return of capital includes distributions paid by a fund in excess of its net investment income. Such excess is distributed from the fund's assets. Under federal tax regulations, some or all of the return of capital distributed by a fund may be taxed as ordinary income. The amount of monthly distributions may vary depending on a number of factors, including changes in portfolio and market conditions.

Cohen & Steers Closed-End Opportunity Fund, Inc., Cohen & Steers Total Return Realty Fund, Inc., Cohen & Steers REIT and Preferred and Income Fund, Inc., Cohen & Steers Quality Income Realty Fund, Inc., and Cohen & Steers Infrastructure Fund, Inc. only:

Cohen & Steers Closed-End Opportunity Fund, Inc., Cohen & Steers Total Return Realty Fund, Inc., Cohen & Steers REIT and Preferred and Income Fund, Inc., Cohen & Steers Quality Income Realty Fund, Inc., and Cohen & Steers Infrastructure Fund, Inc. declared their monthly distributions pursuant to such Funds' managed distribution plans. Each Fund implemented a managed distribution policy in accordance with exemptive relief issued by the Securities and Exchange Commission. The policy gives each Fund greater flexibility to realize long-term capital gains throughout the year and to distribute those gains on a regular monthly basis to shareholders. Information can also be found on the Funds' website at cohenandsteers.com. The Board of Directors of each Fund may amend, terminate or suspend the managed distribution policy at any time, which could have an adverse effect on the market price of each Fund's shares.

Distributions of a Fund's investment in real estate investment trusts (REITs), master limited partnerships (MLPs) and/or closed-end funds (CEFs) may later be characterized as capital gains and/or a return of capital, depending on the character of the dividends reported to each Fund after year-end by the REITs, MLPs and CEFs held by a Fund.

Each Fund's distributions may include net investment income, long-term capital gains, short-term capital gains and/or return of capital. Under the plan, prior to the payment date of the distribution every month, each Fund will issue a press release and a notice containing information about the amount and sources of the distribution and other related information to shareholders of record on the record date. Please note that the notice is not provided for tax reporting purposes but for informational purposes only. Information can also be found on the Funds' website at cohenandsteers.com.

Shareholders should not use the information provided in preparing their tax returns. Shareholders will receive a Form 1099-DIV for the calendar year indicating how to report Fund distributions for federal income tax purposes.

Investors should consider the investment objectives, risks, charges and expense of a fund carefully before investing. You can obtain the Funds' most recent periodic reports, when available, and other regulatory filings by contacting your financial advisor or visiting cohenandsteers.com. These reports and other filings can be found on the Securities and Exchange Commission's EDGAR Database. You should read these reports and other filings carefully before investing.

About Cohen & Steers. Cohen & Steers is a leading global investment manager specializing in real assets and alternative income, including listed and private real estate, preferred securities, infrastructure, resource equities, commodities, as well as multi-strategy solutions. Founded in 1986, the firm is headquartered in New York City, with offices in London, Dublin, Hong Kong, Tokyo and Singapore.

Forward-Looking Statements

This press release and other statements that Cohen & Steers may make may contain forward looking statements within the meaning of Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933 and Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, which reflect the company's current views with respect to, among other things, its operations and financial performance. You can identify these forward-looking statements by the use of words such as "outlook," "believes," "expects," "potential," "continues," "may," "will, " "should," "seeks," "approximately," "predicts," "intends," "plans," "estimates," "anticipates," or the negative versions of these words or other comparable words. Such forward-looking statements are subject to various risks and uncertainties.

Accordingly, there are or will be important factors that could cause actual outcomes or results to differ materially from those indicated in these statements. The company undertakes no obligation to publicly update or review any forward-looking statement, whether as a result of new information, future developments or otherwise.
